items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities, including those leading to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, and security feature bypass. Adobe recommends users update their installations to the newest version.

items.title: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical vulnerability (CVSS 9.6) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source allows for unrestricted file upload, potentially leading to privilege escalation. This issue is addressed in APSB26-73.

items.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This critical vulnerability (CVSS 9.1) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution due to improper encoding or escaping of output. It is part of the APSB26-73 security update.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, which can lead to remote code execution. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: Denial-of-Service Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This critical denial-of-service vulnerability in Adobe Commerce is caused by a dependency on a vulnerable third-party component. Successful exploitation can crash the application, disrupting storefront and administrative availability.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload via REST API (PolyShell)
items.summary: Sansec identified PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images. Attacks have been observed in the wild, and while Magento 2.4.9 contains a fix, older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update addresses multiple vulnerabilities, including security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read. Adobe recommends applying the patches as quickly as possible.
